quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of antisemitic sentiment moved into the board's Leading section, bringing with it a torrent of hate speech. The system's central pressure dropped rapidly as Anon scapegoated Jews for ruining video games, drawing a flurry of responses.

A scattered drizzle of requests for explicit content fell across the Discourse section, with one anon seeking 'lotty chootiepies of bharat' and another looking for a tall, cultured partner. The atmosphere remained overcast with a high chance of lewd replies.

A cold snap of blunt advice chilled the board's Notices section, as one anon told another to 'stop being an asscrack jeet' and invest in perfume.

Meanwhile, a heat wave of indulgence swept through the section, with an anon reporting a binge of vada paavs and lassi.

articles.title: Anon blames Jews for ruining video games
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a one-word post: 'Jews'. One anon responded by bringing up 'GamerGate', a controversy from 2014 involving allegations of sexism and corruption in the video game industry.

This anon claimed it was a 'culture war' that began when a Jewish developer was accused of sleeping with reviewers to get good reviews for her game. Another anon chimed in, stating that 'Jews ruined video games forever'.

This poster claimed they bought an RTX 3050 graphics card to play modern games, but found them to be of poor quality due to 'Jewish influence'.

One anon mentioned that many Indians lost jobs due to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives, which they claimed were introduced by Jews through feminism.

This poster argued that even undeserving individuals were able to secure high-paying jobs by working with Microsoft Office and making PowerPoint presentations. Anon also discussed the influence of Jews in India, with one poster stating that it was 'very high'.

This claim was met with a response that Indian DEI initiatives were simply a copy of Western initiatives.

The topic of specific individuals also came up, with one anon mentioning Neil Druckmann, an Israeli game developer, and another bringing up Ken Levine, the Jewish creator of the Bioshock series.

Anon also revisited the Gamergate controversy, with one poster calling it a 'conspiracy' and another stating that it was always about 'Jews'.

The topic of Zoe Quinn, a developer at the center of Gamergate, was also brought up, with one anon noting that 'Quinn is a Jewish name'.

articles.title: Anon requests lotty chootiepies of bharat
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a request for 'lotty chootiepies of bharat'. One anon responded with a homophobic slur and told another user, who they called 'gobarjeet', to leave the thread.

Another anon mentioned that some people maintain their complexion by staying indoors and exercising. One user suggested creating a separate thread for posting about 'dehatiauntiyo' (a colloquial term).

Anon asked if they could post about 'auntiya' (a colloquial term for an older woman) and described a provocative outfit. One user wondered how some people stay fair-skinned in India's sunny weather.

Anon confessed to having a fetish for 'dehati gobars' (a colloquial term). Anon asked others to contribute and share images, using a homophobic slur.

Other users responded with brief comments, including one who mentioned that someone named 'Jeetas' was wearing a particular type of clothing.

articles.title: Anon seeks tall, cultured partner
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread expressing a desire for a physically active lifestyle that seems to increase their libido, specifically for tall, muscular men.

They mentioned having difficulty meeting such individuals in real life, as most people they encounter are shorter or smaller than them.

One respondent suggested using dating apps like Grindr, but Anon expressed dissatisfaction with such platforms, citing concerns about the type of people they attract. Another anon recommended looking for known queer spots to meet potential partners.

Anon specified a preference for someone who is not only tall, ideally over 6'4, but also cultured and able to engage in meaningful conversation.

They emphasized that physical attributes alone are not enough, valuing qualities like being 'nice and gentle.' The thread also included various personal anecdotes and jokes from other users, with some sharing their own experiences with dating and physical appearance.

One anon humorously described themselves as 'tall but soft and faggy as fuck,' while another joked about the influence of microplastics on sexual orientation.

Anon's query sparked a discussion on meeting queer individuals, with some lamenting the lack of openly queer spaces. The conversation remained focused on the challenges of meeting like-minded people and the importance of cultural compatibility in a partner.

articles.title: Anon tells another to 'stop being an asscrack jeet'
articles.body:
Anon posted about someone being an 'asscrack jeet' and suggested they use perfume. The original poster claimed local shops sell water and advised buying a strong perfume.

One respondent wrote that pajeets are envious and jealous, and will try to bring others down. Another poster mentioned that India has people with parasites controlling their actions, and warned not to trust strangers.

A separate comment joked about removing 'izzat points' from a random person on the street.

articles.title: Anon gorges on vada paavs and lassi
articles.body: Anon reported eating 7 vada paavs and 2 glasses of lassi after a day of fasting. They expressed satisfaction with their meal, calling the lassi 'freaking amazing'. Other anons responded with casual comments, including one who joked about the OP's stomach. One anon asked about the OP's caste. Recommendations for mango shake and aam pana were also made.
